commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Of Juda. Hebrew, "Israel. "But the Septuagint have "Juda. "(Haydock)
The names are often used promiscuously. (Calmet)
For he. Septuagint, "because he or it had apostatised from the Lord. "
Stript. Protestants, "he made Judah naked, and transgressed sore "Thus were the idolatrous Israelites exposed to shame and misery, after they had adored the golden calf, Exodus xxxii. 25. (Haydock)
In the Bacchanalian riots, the pagans threw off their garments, to show that they trusted entirely in their false gods. (Tirinus)
Achaz had plunged his country in distress, (Calmet) both of soul and body, (Haydock) by his scandalous deportment. (Calmet)
Of all help, is added by way of explanation; (Tirinus) as God would no longer rescue his rebellious people from the hands of the enemy. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
